---
title: "About"
toc: false
---

I work at the intersection most people only have one half of: I can **govern data** *and* **technically audit whether an AI system is trustworthy** (fair, transparent, and explainable).

I started in data governance as a Data Steward Facilitator at a 150-branch bank, standing up a brand-new governance function: assigning data ownership, running data stewards, driving data quality, and training the business. Hands-on with Oracle's data-quality and integration stack (EDQ profiling and matching, ODI).

Underneath both is a BSc in Mathematics & Statistics: hypothesis testing, theory of estimation, design and analysis of sample surveys, and quality-control methods. That is the quantitative backbone of audit work: sizing an evaluation, designing an ablation, drawing a defensible sample, and stating "this difference is real, not noise" at a stated confidence level.

Then I went deep on the AI side, with an MSc in Cognitive Systems at the University of Potsdam, expected 2026 (machine learning, NLP, data mining, trustworthy AI). My thesis evaluates whether the chain-of-thought *explanations* a vision–language model produces are actually **faithful** to how it really decided, using a perturbation battery that exposes when models rationalize their own mistakes. My open interpretability portfolio extends this across five projects: faithfulness benchmarking of text explainers, modality attribution for multimodal models, calibration versus accuracy trade-offs of interpretable models on clinical data, and mechanistic probing.

That combination is what the EU AI Act era needs: someone who can take a high-risk AI system and produce the evidence (bias and fairness testing, explanation-faithfulness audits, model documentation, data-quality and lineage controls) that transparency and oversight obligations require, while speaking the language of the data governance teams who own the data.

**Open to:** remote / international roles in AI Governance, Responsible AI, AI Assurance / Model Risk, and technical Data Governance. Based in Berlin.

- Flagship audit: [eu-ai-act-credit-assurance](https://github.com/Desmond-Mariita/eu-ai-act-credit-assurance)
- Interpretability portfolio: [interpretability-foundations](https://github.com/Desmond-Mariita/interpretability-foundations)
